target.title: feat(opencode): add resolveDynamicModel and augmentModelCatalog hooks to Zen plugin
target.body:
## Problem

The `opencode` (Zen) provider plugin, introduced in the OpenCode provider-key split, is missing `resolveDynamicModel` and `augmentModelCatalog` runtime hooks that **every other bundled provider implements**.

Without these hooks, the agent runtime **cannot resolve `opencode/<model-id>` references** at runtime. When a model like `opencode/deepseek-v4-flash-free` is configured in `agents.defaults.models`, the resolution chain:

1. ❌ Checks `modelRegistry.find("opencode", "deepseek-v4-flash-free")` — not found
2. ❌ Calls `plugin.resolveDynamicModel("opencode", "deepseek-v4-flash-free")` — **undefined** (hook not implemented)
3. ❌ Falls through to `resolveConfiguredFallbackModel` — requires explicit `models.providers["opencode"]` config entry
4. ❌ **"Unknown model" error**

Users hit this as `⚠️ Unknown model: opencode/deepseek-v4-flash-free` when trying to use the Zen proxy through fallback chains, requiring fragile config workarounds (manual `models.providers` entries or models.json manipulation) that are not needed for any other provider.

**Severity:** Medium — annoyance that requires manual config work for every Zen model the user wants to reference in the fallback chain.

## Solution

Add runtime hooks to the `opencode` Zen provider plugin, mirroring the pattern established by `opencode-go` at `extensions/opencode-go/provider-catalog.ts`:

### New file: `extensions/opencode/provider-catalog.ts`

Static model catalog with known Zen-proxied models:

| Model | API | Reasoning | Context |
|-------|-----|-----------|---------|
| deepseek-v4-flash-free | openai-completions | true | 65K |
| claude-opus-4-6 | openai-responses | true | 200K |
| claude-opus-4-7 | openai-responses | true | 200K |
| claude-sonnet-4-6 | openai-responses | true | 200K |
| gemini-2.5-pro | openai-responses | true | 1M |

Catalog functions:
- `listOpencodeZenModelCatalogEntries()` — discoverable catalog entries
- `resolveOpencodeZenModel(modelId)` — runtime model resolution
- `normalizeOpencodeZenBaseUrl()` — base URL normalization (accepts `https://opencode.ai`, `https://opencode.ai/zen`, `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1`)
- `normalizeOpencodeZenResolvedModel()` — resolved model base URL normalization

### Modified: `extensions/opencode/index.ts` (+5 hooks)

- `resolveDynamicModel` → delegates to `resolveOpencodeZenModel()`
- `augmentModelCatalog` → delegates to `listOpencodeZenModelCatalogEntries()`
- `normalizeConfig` → normalizes provider config base URLs
- `normalizeResolvedModel` → normalizes resolved model base URLs
- `normalizeTransport` → normalizes transport-level base URLs

### Resolution chain after fix

1. ✅ `modelRegistry.find("opencode", "deepseek-v4-flash-free")` — found (catalog provides it)
2. ✅ `plugin.resolveDynamicModel("opencode", "deepseek-v4-flash-free")` — resolves at runtime
3. (Escape hatch) No config workaround needed

## Verification

### Build & Tests

- `pnpm build` — clean
- `pnpm test -- extensions/opencode` — **17/17 tests passed** (5 test files)

Test output:

```
extension-misc extensions/opencode/index.test.ts (8 tests)
extension-misc extensions/opencode/onboard.test.ts (2 tests)
extension-misc extensions/opencode/provider-policy-api.test.ts (2 tests)
extension-misc extensions/opencode/plugin-registration.contract.test.ts (2 tests)
extension-misc extensions/opencode/media-understanding-provider.test.ts (3 tests)

Test Files  5 passed (5)
     Tests  17 passed (17)
```

### Unit test coverage

- `resolveDynamicModel` resolves `deepseek-v4-flash-free` with correct api/reasoning/context metadata matching the live test
- `resolveDynamicModel` resolves `claude-opus-4-6` with `openai-responses` api
- `resolveDynamicModel` returns `undefined` for unknown model IDs (graceful fallback)
- `augmentModelCatalog` returns entries including both DeepSeek and Claude models

### Live behavior proof

Model discovered through provider catalog after patch:
```
opencode/deepseek-v4-flash-free  STATUS  configured,fallback#2,alias:DeepSeekFree
```

The model resolves at runtime without requiring a `models.providers["opencode"]` entry.

Rebased on `upstream/main` — clean, no merge conflicts. Fork synced.

## Why this matters

The `opencode` Zen provider is a multi-model proxy (Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek). Without `resolveDynamicModel`, every model requires a manual `models.providers` config entry — creating a poor developer experience and ongoing maintenance burden. With the hooks in place, models are automatically discoverable and resolvable, exactly like every other bundled provider.

## Change History

- **v2**: Fixed DeepSeek model metadata to match existing live test: `api: "openai-completions"`, `reasoning: true`, `contextWindow: 65_536`, `maxTokens: 8_192`. Added 4 unit tests for `resolveDynamicModel` and `augmentModelCatalog`.
target.author: olisikh

target.title: test: add temp directory helper guidance
target.body:
## Summary
- Close out the hard cleanup-sufficiency scanner approach from #78416 with a helper-first replacement.
- Extend `test/helpers/temp-dir.ts` with an explicit tracker API and more robust cleanup retries.
- Add a report-only `scripts/report-test-temp-creations.mjs` migration aid that inspects added diff lines for new bare temp-dir creation without judging cleanup data flow.
- Document the recommended temp-dir helper pattern in the testing guide.

## Verification
- `pnpm test test/helpers/temp-dir.test.ts test/scripts/report-test-temp-creations.test.ts`
- `node scripts/report-test-temp-creations.mjs --base origin/main --head HEAD`
- `node scripts/report-test-temp-creations.mjs --help`
- `pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/help/testing.md`
- `pnpm lint:scripts`
- `npm_config_registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/ node scripts/check-changed.mjs`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --parallel-tests "npm_config_registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/ node scripts/check-changed.mjs"` clean: no accepted/actionable findings
target.author: hxy91819
